message.content:
READ-ONLY deep refactoring audit of the Collection Report DETAILS page of the Evolution One CMS (Next.js App Router, MongoDB/Mongoose, TypeScript). DO NOT edit any files. Only read and report.

Entry point: `app/collection-report/report/[id]/page.tsx` (the dynamic segment may be named [reportId] — confirm) and also `app/collection-report/report/session/[sessionId]/page.tsx` if present. Trace the full tree: the PageContent (search components/CMS/collectionReport/), all child components (the report detail view, collection entry tables, add/edit collection modals, machine history sections), all custom hooks (lib/hooks/collectionReport/ — e.g. useNewCollectionModal, edit/mobile collection modal hooks), all API routes called (app/api/collection-reports/[id], app/api/collection-reports/collections/[id], etc.), and all helpers/utils/types (lib/helpers/collectionReport, app/api/lib/helpers, shared/types, lib/types).

DOMAIN INVARIANTS to verify (from CLAUDE.md — critical):
- `isEditing` flag: State 2 (isEditing:true = "Checked Out", machine histories NOT yet synced, unsafe for financial reporting); State 3 (isEditing:false = "Finalized", auditable). Verify the detail/edit flow transitions and that financial reads filter isEditing:false.
- Collection data: locationReportId & isCompleted MUST stay in sync. prevIn/prevOut are BACKEND-calculated — frontend must NEVER send them (check the add/edit collection modal payloads). movement.gross is ground truth, fixed after save; recalculation only permitted in "Add Entry" phase. CRITICAL: updateMachineCollectionData (creation) advances the machine's meter state, but editing a collection entry (PATCH /api/collection-reports/collections/[id]) MUST NOT update machine.collectionMeters — verify the PATCH route does not.
- SMIB online/offline: online SMIB (relayId + recent lastActivity) → relay handles sasMeters, do NOT mutate during CR creation; offline SMIB → update sasMeters.drop/totalCancelledCredits with collector values.
- Gaming day offset: collection reports do NOT apply gaming day offset. Verify.

GENERAL RULES:
- TYPE SAFETY: zero `any`, `type` not `interface`, `.lean<T>()`, no `Record<string,unknown>` for domain data, explicit return types, NO comments in type files.
- NAMING: PageContent suffix, [Page][Section][Component], Props suffix, handle/fetch/format/calculate/map prefixes, no single-letter vars.
- MONGOOSE: `.lean<T>()`, `.aggregate<T>()`, Meters cursor not .exec(), findOne({_id}) not findById, types from shared/types.
- API ROUTES: file-level JSDoc @module, numbered STEP comments ===== separators, withApiAuth, helper extraction >30 lines, route<500/helper<600 lines, perf tracking warn >1000ms, getUserLocationFilter(), reviewer scale via getMoneyInScale/getMoneyOutAndJackpotScale (never inline).
- CURRENCY: never hardcode `$`, formatCurrencyWithCodeString(amount, displayCurrency), pass ?currency=.
- REACT/NEXT: direct React imports, section comments `// =====`, skeletons matching layout (no spinners), financial color helpers, useMemo/useCallback.
- HOOK STRUCTURE: 13-section order (reference: lib/hooks/collectionReport/useNewCollectionModal.ts).
- FILE ORG: shared/types vs lib/types vs app/api/lib/types; redundancy/dup; files over limits.
- SECURITY: location filter, conditional cookie secure, Mongoose models only.
- REDUNDANCY: unused imports/exports/types (grep to confirm), dead code, files to merge/split.

Return:
## Collection Report (Details) Findings
Markdown table: Category | File (path) | Issue | Severity (Critical/High/Med/Low) | Concrete Fix.
## Proposed Actions (ordered, concrete)
## Files read (full list)
Be specific with paths and line numbers. Prioritize real, verifiable issues, ESPECIALLY any isEditing / prevIn-prevOut-from-frontend / PATCH-mutating-machine.collectionMeters / SMIB sasMeters violations. Flag anything you could NOT fully verify. Do NOT make changes.

- api-route-structure: Structure API routes with step-by-step comments, JSDoc, helper extraction, and proper organization.
- backend-standards: Mongoose models over direct collections, findOne() not findById(), licencee filtering, aggregation patterns.
- code-style-eslint: Never ignore ESLint violations, run lint regularly, file-level documentation, remove redundant comments.
- database-query-optimization: Use .cursor() for Meters, direct location field access, eliminate N+1 patterns, handle gaming day offsets.
- file-organization: Directory structure for shared code, frontend, backend, components, and utilities across the application.
- gaming-day-offset-system: 8 AM to 8 AM business day (Trinidad UTC-4), gaming day calculations, custom date handling, per-location offsets.
- licencee-access-control: Multi-tenant access control, role-based permissions, location filtering, session invalidation on permission changes.
- local-app-testing: Logging in and manually testing the Evolution One CMS in a real browser on localhost. Use when driving the app via the browser (Claude in Chrome) to verify a change, test a flow, or reproduce a bug. Contains the dev login credentials.
- page-component-structure: page.tsx thin wrappers, component organization with section comments, and JSX formatting patterns.
- react-typescript-rules: React imports, TypeScript discipline, state management patterns, and type organization rules.
- skeleton-loaders: Content-specific skeleton loaders that exactly match real content layout. NEVER use generic spinners or "Loading..." text.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

2	 * Collection by ID API Route
3	 *
4	 * This route handles updating a specific collection by ID.
5	 * It supports:
6	 * - Updating collection fields (meters, timestamps, notes, etc.)
7	 * - Recalculating movement and SAS metrics when meters change
8	 * - Recalculating SAS time ranges when timestamp changes
9	 * - Updating machine collectionMetersHistory
10	 * - Cascading recalculation to related collections
11	 *
12	 * @module app/api/collection-reports/collections/[id]/route
13	 */
14	
15	import {
16	  cascadeRecalculationForPatch,
17	  determineRecalculationFlags,
18	  extractSasTimeFieldsFromPayload,
19	  handleRamClearToggleWithRelayGuard,
20	  logCollectionPatchActivity,
21	  recalculateMovementAndSasForPatch,
22	  updateMachineHistoryForPatch,
23	} from '@/app/api/lib/helpers/collectionReport/collectionByIdOperations';
24	import { Collections } from '@/app/api/lib/models/collections';
25	import { connectDB } from '@/app/api/lib/middleware/db';
26	import {
27	  logRouteUpdate,
28	  logRouteError,
29	  extractUserFromRequest,
30	} from '@/app/api/lib/utils/routeLogger';
31	import type { CollectionDocument } from '@/lib/types/collection';
32	import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
33	
34	/**
35	 * PATCH /api/collection-reports/collections/[id]
36	 *
37	 * EDITS a single collection document (NOT creation flow).
38	 * Used when user edits a collection entry in the UI to correct values.
39	 *
40	 * Key behaviors:
41	 * - Updates collection document with edited values (metersIn, metersOut, prevIn, prevOut, notes, etc.)
42	 * - Recalculates movement and SAS metrics when meters change
43	 * - Recalculates SAS time ranges when timestamp changes
44	 * - Updates machine collectionMetersHistory entry
45	 * - Does NOT update machine.collectionMeters (only creation flow does this)
46	 * - Calls updateRegularAndRamClearMeters with fully updated document
47	 *
48	 * @see updateMachineCollectionData — for CREATION flow (updates machine meters)
49	 *
50	 * @param {string} id - Required (path). The _id of the collection to update.
51	 *
52	 * Body fields:
53	 * @param {number} [metersIn] - Optional. New meter-in reading. Triggers movement
54	 *                                            recalculation and cascade.
55	 * @param {number} [metersOut] - Optional. New meter-out reading. Triggers movement
56	 *                                            recalculation and cascade.
57	 * @param {string|Date} [sasEndTime] - Optional. Override for sasMeters.sasEndTime.
58	 * @param {string|Date} [sasStartTime] - Optional. Override for sasMeters.sasStartTime.
59	 * @param {string|Date} [timestamp] - Optional. Collection timestamp.
60	 * @param {string|Date} [collectionTime] - Optional. Alternative collection timestamp field.
61	 * @param {boolean} [ramClear] - Optional. Flags that a RAM-clear occurred.
62	 * @param {number} [ramClearMetersIn] - Optional. Post-RAM-clear meter-in value.
63	 * @param {number} [ramClearMetersOut] - Optional. Post-RAM-clear meter-out value.
64	 * @param {number} [ramClearCoinIn] - Optional. Post-RAM-clear coin-in value.
65	 * @param {number} [ramClearCoinOut] - Optional. Post-RAM-clear coin-out value.
66	 * @param {string} [notes] - Optional. Free-text notes on the collection entry.
67	 * @param {number} [prevIn] - Optional. Override for the previous meter-in value.
68	 * @param {number} [prevOut] - Optional. Override for the previous meter-out value.
69	 *
70	 * Note: collector and collectorName fields are intentionally stripped before the update
71	 * to prevent accidental reassignment of collection ownership.
72	 *
73	 * Flow:
74	 * 1. Parse route parameters and request body
75	 * 2. Validate collection ID
76	 * 3. Connect to database and fetch original collection
77	 * 4. Handle RAM clear toggle (relay-aware)
78	 * 5. Remove immutable _id, extract SAS time fields
79	 * 6. Build and apply first update (basic fields + explicit SAS times)
80	 * 7. Recalculate movement and SAS metrics if needed
81	 * 8. Update machine collectionMetersHistory
82	 * 9. Cascade recalculation to related collections
83	 * 10. Log activity and return updated collection
84	 */
85	export async function PATCH(request: NextRequest) {
86	  const startTime = Date.now();
87	  const functionName = 'PATCH /api/collection-reports/collections/[id]';
88	  const user = extractUserFromRequest(request);
89	  const { pathname } = request.nextUrl;
90	  const collectionId = pathname.split('/').pop();
91	
92	  try {
93	    // ============================================================================
94	    // STEP 1: Parse request body
95	    // ============================================================================
96	    const updateData = await request.json();
97	
98	    // ============================================================================
99	    // STEP 2: Validate collection ID
100	    // ============================================================================
101	    if (!collectionId) {
102	      logRouteError(
103	        functionName,
104	        'PATCH',
105	        '/api/collection-reports/collections/[id]',
106	        'Collection ID is required',
107	        user
108	      );
109	      return NextResponse.json(
110	        { success: false, error: 'Collection ID is required' },
111	        { status: 400 }
112	      );
113	    }
114	
115	    // ============================================================================
116	    // STEP 3: Connect to database and fetch original collection
117	    // ============================================================================
118	    await connectDB();
119	
120	    const originalCollection = await Collections.findOne({

143	    // ============================================================================
144	    const { _id, ...safeUpdateData } = updateData as Record<string, unknown>;

147	    }

151	
152	    // ============================================================================

154	    // ============================================================================
155	    const updatePayload: Record<string, unknown> = {
156	      ...safeUpdateData,

158	    };
159	
160	    if (sasFields.hasPayloadSasEndTime) {

171	    const updateQuery: Record<string, unknown> = {
172	      $set: updatePayload,

Critical observation: the edit modal's "Add new entry" path (line 1322) POSTs to `/api/collection-reports/collections` with `prevIn`/`prevOut` from the frontend (lines 1277-1278), and the edit path (line 1011) also sends `prevIn`/`prevOut`. Per CLAUDE.md "Never send prevIn/prevOut from the frontend — the backend calculates these." This is a violation worth flagging — though the local CLAUDE.md (push-forward) shows the edit flow does accept prevIn from payload. Let me verify the `recalculateMachineCollections` to settle the `collectionMeters` mutation question, and check the GET enrichment for isEditing filtering and reviewer scale. Let me also confirm whether the `collections` POST route accepts prevIn.
